---
title: "Churn Analysis for Bank Customers"
description: "Using Python to predict customer churn in the banking sector, leveraging machine learning techniques and data visualisation."
author: "Alina Bolshakova"
date: 2026-04-10
image: /images/project_1.png
categories: [Python, Machine Learning, Data Visualisation]
code-fold: true
code-tools: true
---

## Overview

This project analyses customer churn in the banking sector — identifying key factors that influence whether a customer leaves their bank. Using machine learning models, we predict churn risk and provide actionable insights for customer retention.

{{< fa brands github >}} [View full notebook on GitHub](https://github.com/AyaBlck/Python_Projects/blob/main/bank_churn.ipynb){target=_blank}

## Data Loading & Exploration

```{python}
#| error: true

import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')
sns.set_style('whitegrid')

bank_data = pd.read_csv("bank_new_dataset.csv")
bank_data.head()
```

## Data Cleaning

```{python}
#| error: true

b_data = bank_data[(bank_data['balance'] < 2300000) | (bank_data['balance'].isna())]
b_data = b_data[b_data['salary'] < 300000]
b_data.head()
```

## Key Findings

::: {.callout-tip}
## Main Insights
- Older customers with higher balances were more likely to churn
- Credit score alone was not a strong predictor of churn
- Tenure played a significant role in customer retention
:::

## Methodology

The analysis followed these steps:

1. Data cleaning and preprocessing
2. Exploratory data analysis with interactive visualisations
3. Feature engineering
4. Model training and evaluation

> "The goal is not just to predict churn, but to understand *why* customers leave."

## Results

---

*This project demonstrates the application of data science techniques to solve real-world business problems in the financial sector.*
